twitter.tone: punchy and direct — highlight urgency, frustration with the market, and instant value

reddit.tone: casual and helpful, like a real person who built something to solve their own problem — never salesy, always add value to the conversation first

facebook.tone: friendly and conversational — empathise with the rental struggle, position as a free tool from the community

linkedin.tone: professional but personable — builder sharing their product journey, the problem they noticed, and how they solved it; engage with Irish tech and property communities

strategy_notes.general_approach: Lead with empathy for the Irish rental crisis. Everyone knows listings vanish in minutes — position RentPulse as the unfair advantage. Emphasise FREE and instant.
strategy_notes.twitter_strategy: Short punchy threads about how fast Irish rentals disappear. Use stats (e.g. 'Average Daft listing gets 100+ enquiries in 1 hour'). Engage with housing journalists, TDs, and rental commentators. Quote-tweet Daft reports and housing news with a subtle CTA.
strategy_notes.reddit_strategy: Never post direct ads. Comment helpfully in rental-seeking threads. Share as 'I built this free tool' in relevant threads. Do a proper launch post in r/ireland or r/DevelEire framed as a side project. Answer questions transparently.
strategy_notes.facebook_strategy: Post in accommodation groups when people express frustration about missing listings. Share as a helpful recommendation, not an ad. Create short video demos showing how fast alerts come through. Encourage word of mouth.
strategy_notes.linkedin_strategy: Frame as a build-in-public story. Share the journey: why you built it, the tech stack, user feedback. Tag Irish tech influencers and proptech accounts. Post milestone updates (user counts, listings tracked).
strategy_notes.content_cadence: Twitter: 1-2 posts daily plus engagement. Reddit: 3-4 helpful comments per week, 1 post per fortnight. Facebook: 2-3 group posts per week, rotate groups. LinkedIn: 2-3 posts per week.
key_hooks: Daft listings disappear in minutes — get alerts before everyone else
Free Chrome extension — no catch, no subscription
Built by Irish renters, for Irish renters
The rental market is broken — this levels the playing field
strategy_notes.timing_note: All times in Irish Standard Time (IST/GMT+1 in summer, GMT in winter). Peak engagement aligns with commute times, lunch breaks, and evening browsing.
